sections.heading: An Act to provide income tax deductions for
sections.title: Tax deductions for film licensed investment company shares
sections.summary: This section amends the Taxation Laws to allow deductions for shares in film licensed investment companies (FLICs) issued during the period the FLIC's concessional capital licence is active. Deductions apply only to shares issued before July 1, 2000, and are subject to specific conditions regarding the FLIC's licence status and the timing of share issuance. The deduction is available to individuals and partnerships holding shares in FLICs, with special rules for partners and partnerships.

requirements.text: You can deduct money paid for shares in a film licensed investment company issued to you by the FLIC during the period the FLIC's concessional capital licence is in force.
A film licensed investment company is a company granted a licence to raise concessional capital under the Film Licensed Investment Company Act 1998.
You lose entitlement to the deduction if the Arts Minister decides to remove the concessional status of your shares.

You lose entitlement to the deduction if the Commissioner is satisfied the FLIC breached conditions under the Film Licensed Investment Company Act 1998 and the Arts Minister has not acted within 6 months.
The Commissioner must notify the Arts Minister within 28 days if the Commissioner is satisfied of the matters in subsection (2).
If you lose entitlement after claiming the deduction, your assessment may be amended to disallow the deduction.

sections.source_quote: You can deduct amounts you have paid for shares issued to you by a film licensed investment company (FLIC). The deduction does not apply to shares issued after 1 July 2000.
